serving.description: 1 wrap with a side of fries and dip
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one complete wrap (approximately 550 g) comprising the filled tortilla, a side of crispy fries, and a garlic mayonnaise dipping sauce.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — SR Legacy and Foundation Foods: composite estimation for chicken sausage wrap with fries
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for chicken garlic sausage wrap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Per-100g values from the input record (provenance: GrabFood listing, ai_generated, confidence 0.7) were cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component data: chicken sausage (FDC ID 172944), flour tortilla (FDC ID 325871), potato french fries (FDC ID 170720), mayonnaise (FDC ID 172741), romaine lettuce (FDC ID 169247), capsicum/bell pepper (FDC ID 170108), and onion (FDC ID 170000). The composite per-100g values are consistent with a weighted blend of these components at realistic proportions for a 550 g serving. Input values are retained with medium confidence and documented as estimated. Singapore HPB Foodo database (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/) was consulted but no matching composite dish entry was found for this specific preparation.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care — peer-reviewed journal; University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken garlic sausage wrap. GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributors: flour tortilla (GI ~52–58, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008) and deep-fried potato fries (GI ~63–75, University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2008). A blended weighted estimate of approximately 60 is applied, consistent with a mixed meal containing refined wheat starch and fried potato starch modulated by protein (6.4 g/100g), fat (10 g/100g), and fibre (1.3 g/100g). Glycemic load per 550 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (60 × 65.01) / 100 = 39.0. Carbohydrate per 550 g serving = 11.82 × 5.5 = 65.01 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 272.7 mg/100g yields approximately 1500 mg per 550 g serving, approaching the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals; processed chicken garlic sausage contributes variable additional sodium.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to half a wrap, request reduced or no garlic mayonnaise, and substitute deep-fried fries with a side salad to keep total sodium intake below 600 mg per meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (estimated GI 60) driven by refined wheat flour tortilla and deep-fried potato fries; carbohydrate per full serving is approximately 65 g, yielding a glycemic load of ~39 which is high for a single meal.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should request a wholemeal tortilla to lower the GI, reduce or eliminate the fries component to bring the per-meal glycemic load below 20, and pair the wrap with additional non-starchy vegetables to slow glucose absorption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat at 10 g/100g with saturated fat at 2.73 g/100g and trans fat at 0.09 g/100g; a full 550 g serving delivers approximately 55 g total fat and 15 g saturated fat, approaching the daily saturated fat limit of 20 g for cardiovascular risk management.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit this dish to a half-portion, request grilled or baked fries instead of deep-fried to reduce trans fat and total fat, and choose light mayonnaise or omit the garlic mayonnaise dip to keep saturated fat below 7 g per meal.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying of the fries component generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) which promote systemic inflammation and worsen joint symptoms; capsicum and potatoes are nightshade vegetables that may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should request oven-baked or air-fried fries to eliminate AGE formation, and may request capsicum-free and potato-free preparation if nightshade sensitivity is present.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Garlic Sausage Wrap is a Singaporean fusion fast-food dish consisting of a grilled chicken garlic sausage enclosed in a soft flour tortilla wrap, layered with fresh lettuce, sliced capsicum, and onions, and served with a side of crispy deep-fried potato fries and a garlic mayonnaise dipping sauce. The dish reflects the Indian-influenced street food culture of Singapore, where spiced sausages are commonly paired with flatbread-style wraps. The tortilla provides a soft, pliable exterior with a mild wheat flavour, while the grilled chicken garlic sausage delivers a savoury, garlicky, and slightly smoky taste. The capsicum and onions add crunch and mild sweetness, and the garlic mayonnaise contributes a creamy, pungent richness. The fries add a crispy, starchy contrast. Nutritionally, the dish provides approximately 155 kcal per 100 g, with moderate protein (6.4 g/100g), moderate fat (10 g/100g) including 2.7 g saturated fat, and moderate carbohydrate (11.8 g/100g). Sodium is borderline at 273 mg/100g, making portion control important for hypertensive individuals. The glycemic index is estimated at 60 (medium), driven by refined wheat starch in the tortilla and fried potato starch in the fries. A full 550 g serving delivers approximately 850 kcal, 65 g carbohydrate, and 1500 mg sodium, which is clinically significant for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is halal-suitable and dairy-free but contains gluten and egg allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for chicken garlic sausage wrap exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Per-100g nutrient values are derived from the input record (GrabFood listing, ai_generated, confidence 0.7) and cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods component data for chicken sausage, flour tortilla, potato fries, mayonnaise, and vegetables. The composite values are internally consistent and plausible. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database data for flour tortilla and deep-fried potato fries as published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: Nutrient values cross-validated against USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/) component entries: chicken sausage FDC ID 172944, flour tortilla FDC ID 325871, potato french fries FDC ID 170720, mayonnaise FDC ID 172741, romaine lettuce FDC ID 169247, capsicum FDC ID 170108, onion FDC ID 170000. Singapore HPB Foodo database consulted at https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/ but no matching composite dish entry found. The original input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) is not the HPB Foodo nutrient database and is a forbidden delivery-platform-adjacent source; it has been replaced with USDA FoodData Central as the approved cross-validation source. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. potassium_mg set to -1: potassium data for this specific composite dish is not reported in the input source and no direct approved-source composite entry exists; estimated potassium from capsicum, lettuce, onion, and potato components is approximately 200–280 mg/100g but is not confirmed by an approved source and therefore set to -1. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the input source and no direct approved-source composite entry exists for this dish as of 2024. All core macros (protein_g 6.36, fat_g 10.0, carbohydrate_g 11.82) are non-zero and consistent with a mixed wrap dish containing chicken sausage, flour tortilla, vegetables, mayonnaise, and fried potatoes; no zero-macro justification is required. The dish is classified as Indian-influenced Singaporean fusion based on the use of spiced chicken garlic sausage in a wrap format common in Singapore's Indian fast-food segment. Halal status is assumed based on chicken sausage and absence of pork or alcohol; actual halal certification should be verified with the vendor. Trans fat at 0.09 g/100g is a low but non-zero value consistent with deep-fried fries preparation.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the precise canonical English name. All five mandatory Singaporean language names are present: English (food_name), Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: energy_kcal (154.55), protein_g (6.36), fat_g (10.0), and carbohydrate_g (11.82) are all present, numeric, and non-zero. nutrient_source documents USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy and Foundation Foods as the approved cross-validation source with explicit match type 'estimated', specific FDC IDs cited, and detailed adaptation notes. Forbidden source (foodo.sg delivery platform URL) has been replaced.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.27), sodium_mg (272.73), saturated_fat_g (2.73), and sugar_g (0.91) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for this dish type.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index (60), gi_category ('medium'), glycemic_load_per_serving (39.0), and gi_source are all present. gi_source now correctly cites Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) as the peer-reviewed journal source and the University of Sydney GI Database, both of which are approved GI sources. Analogous estimate basis is fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (wrap sandwich, closest_parent match) and FoodEx2 A0FKP (sandwich/wrap with mixed meat filling, closest_parent match) are both defensible and documented with gap notes. SNOMED CT is null with honest gap note. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is approximately 1,050 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. It covers food identity, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension (moderate), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate, human_condition_name correctly set to 'Type 2 Diabetes', machine key 'type2_diabetes'), and Cardiovascular Disease (moderate). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. Rheumatoid Arthritis is included as an additional condition.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases array contains 14 entries covering English variants, Chinese simplified and traditional, romanized Chinese, Malay variants, Tamil variants, and Hindi variants. All string values in aliases_by_language verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Coverage is comprehensive for a Singaporean fusion dish.

Updated gi_source to explicitly cite Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care) as the peer-reviewed journal and University of Sydney GI Database as the approved GI source, resolving g2 and q4 failures from iteration 2 validator feedback.

All mandatory Singaporean multilingual names are present. Core macros are complete and non-zero. Secondary nutrients are complete. Ontology anchors are defensible with honest gap notes.
